node.text:
Wake up. Go Skate. All Love. No Hate.

Proudly introducing our latest SB Dunk partnership with @froskate, a collective founded by black women and lead by queer skateboarders based in Chicago, IL. 

Founder Karlie Thornton’s mission is to provide inclusive experiences, resources and equity for the BIPOC, non-traditional skateboarding community.

“When I started froSkate in 2019, we didn’t have a business plan, mission statement, or even a goal. It was just me and some friends wanting to learn to skate with people who look like us. We invited friends, started a group chat, and it caught a lot of attention. It showed me there was a need for this in the community. Chicago hadn’t had anything like it before.” — Karlie Thornton (@adobewankenobii), President + Founder

“One thing we’ve always asked of locals and more seasoned skaters is to help welcome folks of our community into the park. Something as simple as a head nod or introduction goes a long way for those who might feel nervous or intimidated when entering the park. It’s incredibly comforting and helpful to be acknowledged and accepted at first sight.” — Lauren “L Brew” Brew (@lo.snap), Vice President

Watch the full video and continue reading our interview with Karlie and L Brew at the 🔗 in bio to learn about their journey in skateboarding, founding froSkate, and turning their collective creative into a video and shoe that reflects the soul of froSkate and the community they serve in Chicago and beyond.

See more at 🔗 in bio.

node.text:
Three riders. Three different perspectives. Through the Hearts is a look at Copenhagen Open through the eyes (and ❤️-shaped sunglasses) of @villewester, @schairerin, and @prod.

Tune in next Wednesday and Friday to hear from Lea and Paul.

node.text:
Our latest Not Here By Luck episode follows @juliebetrix through Annecy, France as she avoids the blistering summer heat and talks about being the first female sponsored by her local shop, @abs_annecy.

Continue watching at 🔗 in bio.

node.text:
Emigrating from Afghanistan to Berlin in 2016, Melika and Mobina Nazari found community and acceptance through skateboarding.⁠
⁠
Our latest Not Here By Luck episode takes a candid look at the sisters’ journeys to new places and challenges faced.⁠
⁠
Learn more and continue watching at the 🔗 in our bio.⁠
⁠
@melliguam @mobina.nazari01

node.text:
Special delivery from the shop serving New York City skateboarding since 2001, @kcdcskateshop.

The SB Dunk High by KCDC is available Friday, April 29 at KCDC and Saturday, April 30 exclusively in select skate shops.

Learn more at 🔗 bio.

node.accessibility_caption: Photo shared by Nike Skateboarding on January 26, 2026 tagging @bronxgirlsskate. May be an image of one or more people, newspaper, street, newsagent, curb and text.

node.text:
BX all day.

Keeping it classic with a foundational black and white colorway, the Bronx Girls Skate SB Dunk Low is constructed with a luxe stingray texture pebbled leather and white Swoosh and pulls in color hits from the NYC train lines BXGS use to navigate the city.

The SB Dunk Low by @bronxgirlsskate is available now in select skate shops and January 27 in SNKRS.

Learn more at NikeSB.com.

📸 @pinkyswearxo

Behind the Design with @uncouthyouth, @h0lymel, and the @bronxgirlsskate SB Dunk Low.

Full video now playing on NikeSB.com.

node.accessibility_caption: Photo shared by Nike Skateboarding on January 22, 2026 tagging @bronxgirlsskate. May be an image of trainers and text that says "NIKE SB NIKE @RONXGIRLSSKATE 의지미의".

node.text:
Founded in 2020 by Mel Ramirez and Kava Vasquez to give the thriving women’s skate scene at River Avenue Skate Park their gold, @bronxgirlsskate's grassroots approach continues to carve out new spaces throughout New York City.

For their first collaboration with Nike SB, the Bronx Girls Skate Dunk Low reinterprets the iconic colors of NYC transit with an Uptown homage to the Boogie Down.

The SB Dunk Low by Bronx Girls Skate drops January 23 in select skate shops and January 27 in SNKRS.

node.text:
Turning the tables.

Nike SB investigates @nardwuar's broadcast legacy with a signature SB Dunk Low featuring a full-coverage tartan construction that’s a one-to-one match of the iconic hat Nardwuar has worn throughout his decades of journalism.

Releasing December 6 in select skate shops.

Hit the bio link to learn more.

node.text:
🎤 Doot doola doot doo…Nardwuar’s got a shoe!

We are hitting the road with The Human Serviette in anticipation of the upcoming Nardwuar SB Dunk release and could not be more excited to see all of you.

Stay tuned to @kingswell_losfeliz, @furnaceskate, @adriftshop, @bluetilelounge, @slamcityskates, @route_one, @laborskateshop, @menuskateshop and @nardwuar’s channels for more info soon.

We hope to see 🫵 there!

#dootdoo

🖌️ @mr_ryanduggan
